subject: chore(db): drop unused legacy tables from remote
body:
Ran the DDL from 20260301231928_drop_unused_tone_and_reply_tables.sql
against remote via Management API. The migration had been recorded as
stub/applied in schema_migrations but the DDL never actually ran, so
tone_usage, message_replies, global_tone_popularity, and the
increment_tone_usage() function persisted as dead storage (~184 kB).

Pre-flight dependency check confirmed no foreign FKs point at any of
the three tables — only self-contained dependencies (the view on
tone_usage, and the function on tone_usage). Drop order followed the
migration file: view, function, then the two tables. Post-drop check
confirmed all four objects absent; all live synctone tables intact;
sibling project's sancio_* tables untouched.

Left the schema_migrations row for 20260301231928 as stub — it records
the migration as applied (now true) and flipping it would diverge from
the stub-name pattern of every other pre-2026-04-21 synctone row. The
CLI uses the version key, not the name, to decide re-apply.

Tags: database, cleanup, migration, docs
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 2
Impact: infrastructure
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: shared-db-hygiene
Semver: patch
